query: What is the representative of semantic segmentation model mentioned in the document?
ground_truth_answer: LangChain RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter and SemanticChunker (based on embedding similarity).

It is a method that uses fixed block length with an overlap step, e.g., block length 500 tokens, overlap 100 tokens, so the next block includes the tail of the previous block to mitigate semantic breakage.

query: Compare fixed-length chunking and separator-based chunking in terms of semantic integrity.
ground_truth_answer: Fixed-length chunking has poor semantic integrity because it cuts sentences arbitrarily, while separator-based chunking preserves natural boundaries (sentences, paragraphs) but may produce uneven chunk lengths.
